product.name: ArAir Mesh AA 250
product.familyDescriptorPl: Dzianiny dystansowe 3D na siedziska i obuwie
product.familyDescriptorEn: 3D spacer mesh for seating and footwear
descriptions: 100% polyester, 250 g/m² 3d warp knitted air mesh fabric.
ArAir Mesh AA 250 fabric due to its 3d warp knitting structure has high suspension function.
It can be used as upholstery mesh for office furniture.
Suitable for sport and military shoes.
One of the usage of ArAir Mesh AA 250 is shock absorber in medical.
technicalParameters: Dimensinal Change | -5% < Length < +5% ; -5% < Width < +5% | TS EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Explicit statement of medical shock-absorber usage.
Product description explicitly states suitability for military shoes.
Product description explicitly states suitability for sport shoes.
Upholstery use is mentioned only for office furniture, not vehicles or vessels.

evidence: Suitable for sport and military shoes.
3D Air Mesh for Shoes (application image label)
primary.rationale: The fabric is explicitly marketed and imaged as a 3D air mesh for shoes, with footwear listed as a core application alongside upholstery and bags, making footwear the most specific and directly evidenced use.

evidence: It can be used as upholstery mesh for office furniture.
secondary.rationale: The product data explicitly and independently states use as upholstery for office furniture, a non-vehicle interior application distinct from footwear.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Only a single incidental mention of shock absorption in medical context; not the dominant or independently developed use.
Mentioned only as one of several shoe end-uses (military shoes), not a standalone military product application.
Mentioned only as one of several shoe end-uses (sport shoes), not a distinct sportswear application.
Upholstery use is explicitly limited to office furniture, not vehicles or vessels, so transport is excluded per rule 10/12.
